title: Select a window to control spectral leakage
description: Match the analysis window to whether you need to resolve close tones or measure amplitude accurately, trading main-lobe width against side-lobe level.
body: Use when computing a spectrum from a non-periodic block where tones do not land on exact bins. Do not leave a rectangular window in place when amplitude accuracy matters, because its high side lobes smear energy. Actions: compare a Hann or Blackman-Harris window against the rectangular case, inspect side-lobe suppression, and record the scalloping loss. Validation signals include a synthetic two-tone test where the chosen window resolves both peaks and amplitude error stays within the spec. Risk boundary: when both resolution and amplitude are critical, escalate to multi-window or flat-top analysis rather than overclaiming a single window's accuracy.
